$comment: RAG index for Greptile — maps a glob scope to the internal doc it should load for context. Copy to .greptile/files.json, then replace entries with your actual internal docs (architecture guides, security runbooks, domain glossaries). Greptile loads the matched doc when reviewing a file under that glob, instead of relying on training data alone.

entries.$comment: Example — point this at your actual API conventions doc
Example — migration safety rules, index policy, rollback expectations
Example — design system rules, accessibility baseline

$note: Keep this list in sync manually — it is not auto-generated from a single source of truth. If your repo already has a canonical rules directory, treat this file as a thin routing table pointing INTO it, not a copy of its content.
